target: Re-evaluate EXP-183 author_* schema extension (ISS-1130, ISS-1147) and rendering decision (ISS-1164) in light of 30-day static state of dome machine-layer.
recommendation: DEFER-AND-DOWNSCOPE: do not implement the 6-field schema extension as designed; replace with a static Part 4.6.x prose subsection enumerating the 9 classified entries and the label-ambiguity disambiguators. Set a re-evaluation trigger keyed on the dome's machine-layer state.
rationale_summary: The dome's machine-layer artifacts (ai_manifest.json, api/current/claim_index.json, methodology.json) have ALL been stamped last_updated:2026-04-17 since one day before EXP-183 was authored. Over a 30-day observation window, the author has classified zero additional WINs in claim_index.json. EXP-183's schema design also referenced two per-entry fields that do not exist in the actual dome data (registry_status, strict_prospective_confirmed) — both must come from other surfaces or be synthesized, which the original proposal did not acknowledge. The "natural-expansion property" rationale (if the author classifies more, slots auto-fill) has empirically failed to materialize. Engineering a 6-field schema extension + 60-row backfill + rendering pipeline change to surface 7-WIN + 2-prediction classifications that the author has shown no intent to grow is over-investment.

evidence_pulled.observation: Top-level last_updated:2026-04-17, version:V51.1. Same date as EXP-183 authoring (2026-04-18). counts: confirmed=69, prospective_confirmed=10, refined=4, registry_accuracy_pct=94.5.

last_updated:2026-04-17, version:V51.1, 9 claim entries (top-level key is claims not entries — schema spec correction). 7 map to WINs (069,068,058x2,011,056,062) and 2 are predictions (pred-eclipse-tier3, pred-solar-009). Class distribution: retrospective_structural=4, supportive_nonunique=1, pending_contested=2, prospective_confirmed=1, open_refinement=1.
last_updated:2026-04-17, version:V51.1. The 5-class taxonomy definitions match what EXP-183 referenced.

evidence_pulled.claim: top-level claim_index.json returns 404 (EXP-183 referenced this path)
evidence_pulled.observation: HTTP 404 — GitHub Pages "File not found". The actual current location is api/current/claim_index.json. EXP-183 schema_extension_proposal references the wrong path (predictions/claim_index.json) — should be predictions/api/current/claim_index.json. Minor but worth fixing in any followup.

evidence_pulled.claim: EXP-183 schema fields registry_status and strict_prospective_confirmed do not exist as per-entry fields in dome data
evidence_pulled.source: Union of all entry keys across the 9 claims in claim_index.json
evidence_pulled.observation: Actual entry keys: claim_class, claim_key, current_best_entry, data_assets, domain, formula_ids, honest_caveat, source_ids, status, summary, supporting_pages, title, visible_label, why_it_matters. The entry has "status" (e.g., "confirmed") — NOT "registry_status". There is no strict_prospective_confirmed boolean at the entry level anywhere in dome's machine layer. The ai_manifest.json top-level count "prospective_confirmed: 10" is an aggregate, not a per-WIN tag.

evidence_pulled.claim: registry_integrity_notes has shrunk in scope versus EXP-183 framing
evidence_pulled.observation: Only 2 entries, both visible_label disambiguators (WIN-058 split into win-058-bedrock-kappa and win-058-theta-rule; WIN-062 normalized to win-062-tesla-wave-speed). These are not substantive per-claim integrity findings; they are renaming aids for API consumers. EXP-183's author_registry_integrity_note field would populate to non-null on at most 4 WIN rows (WIN-058 x2, WIN-062 x1, with the renamed cluster pointers), and the content would not be analytically useful for readers.

evidence_pulled.claim: Author has not classified additional WINs in the 30-day window since EXP-183
evidence_pulled.source: comparison of claim count at EXP-183 authoring (9) and now (9)
evidence_pulled.observation: EXP-183 stated "Only 9 of 69+ claims are currently classified as of 2026-04-18". Today: 9 of 71. Net author classifications added in 30 days: zero (and total WIN count grew by 2, so the unclassified fraction worsened slightly).

schema_spec_corrections_to_EXP_183: TOP-LEVEL KEY: entries[] -> claims[] (claim_index.json top-level wraps the array under "claims")

FIELD: entries[].registry_status (referenced as the source for author_registry_status) DOES NOT EXIST. The closest field is claims[].status (values include "confirmed"). This field is mostly redundant with the visible badge.

FIELD: entries[].strict_prospective_confirmed (referenced as the source for author_strict_prospective_confirmed) DOES NOT EXIST anywhere in the dome's machine layer at the per-entry level. The aggregate count is in ai_manifest.json.counts.prospective_confirmed (currently 10).
FIELD: registry_integrity_notes (referenced as source for author_registry_integrity_note) now contains only 2 disambiguator entries, not per-claim integrity findings.

proposed_resolution.iss_1130: Close with disposition "do not implement schema extension as designed; superseded by static-prose treatment per EXP-395".
proposed_resolution.iss_1147: Close as duplicate of ISS-1130 (same scope, same disposition).
proposed_resolution.iss_1164: Close as obviated: with no schema extension, there is no per-WIN-card empty-field to render. The rendering concern resolves by not-doing.

static_prose_replacement_proposed.placement: Appended as a new subsection inside Part 4.6 Registry Framing Audit (or Timestamp Error tab callout if Part 4.6 has been retired — check current sections.json layout before integration).
content_outline: Lead: in plain English, the dome publishes a 5-class claim taxonomy in api/current/claim_index.json. As of <today>, 9 of 71 visible WIN claims (12.7%) are classified; the file has been stamped last_updated:2026-04-17 for 30 days.

Table: nine entries with claim_key, visible_label, claim_class, and author summary phrase. Bound to current data; if numbers drift this should auto-refresh next analyst run (low-priority maintenance task: poller could grep last_updated and notify when it changes).

Kernel of truth: the taxonomy itself is reasonable. retrospective_structural, supportive_nonunique, pending_contested, prospective_confirmed, open_refinement is a defensible category set for the work. Acknowledge that.

Then the closer: a definition is not an audit. The author has classified the 9 highest-attention claims and stopped. Net new classifications in the 30-day window since the taxonomy went live: zero. The taxonomy is performative, not operational — it lives in the API surface as evidence of methodological seriousness without backing it with the per-claim discipline a real audit would require.
Trigger: if the dome's claim_index.json grows to >20 classified WINs OR its last_updated stamp moves forward, this subsection should be re-evaluated and the schema-extension path reconsidered.
static_prose_replacement_proposed.rendering: Static HTML with <details>/<summary>/ps-summary wrapper per progressive-disclosure spec. No generate-html.js change required.

re_evaluation_trigger.if_either: Re-open the schema-extension question. The natural-expansion premise is testable: a moving claim_index is the trigger that justifies the engineering cost.
re_evaluation_trigger.monitoring_owner: poller (existing daily run) — add a watcher in monitor/poller logic per a separate ISS if this EXP-395 is accepted.

counter_argument_considered: A reviewer might argue that even a static dataset is worth surfacing per-WIN: readers won't find the API surface on their own. Response: the static prose treatment in Part 4.6.x accomplishes that visibility for the 9 actually-classified WINs without paying schema cost for the 62 unclassified ones. The schema-extension upside scales with classification density; until density rises, the per-WIN-card treatment is over-engineering. The Part 4.6.x subsection is the right level of visibility for the current data state.
newsworthy: Dome author published a 5-class claim taxonomy then classified nothing new for 30 days — methodology theatre, not audit

completion_signal.summary_for_decider: EXP-395 recommends declining the EXP-183 schema extension. Dome machine-layer has been static at 2026-04-17 for 30 days; classification count unchanged (9). Two EXP-183 schema fields (registry_status, strict_prospective_confirmed) reference data that does not exist as per-entry fields in the dome's actual claim_index. Replace with a 280-word static Part 4.6.x subsection enumerating the 9 entries plus a moving-target trigger that re-opens the schema question if the dome's machine layer becomes active. Closing all three ISSs together: 1130 (schema) and 1147 (duplicate) close as superseded; 1164 (rendering) closes as obviated.

override_note: Tracker entry baby-orphan-ISS-1130 has review_class:deep-attack. I preserved that classification because the work involved was substantive judgment under PROP-025 (new recommendation declining EXP-183 scope, schema-spec corrections, replacement-prose design, trigger-condition design) — not a value-fact-check verification. Curmudgeon re-review should be a singleton.
